aboutHero.hookSub: Think of me as the person who makes brands sound human instead of corporate. Especially when they’re talking to people who can smell inauthenticity from a mile away.

personalBits.text: I run on cà phê sữa đá and can debate gaming for hours.
I dream of having croissants in Paris one day. Until then, bánh mì chả in Hanoi hits different.

approach.headline: Marketing isn’t about winning customers.
approach.subheadline: It’s about understanding people well enough to actually help them.
paragraphs: My approach is deeply inspired by Hùng Võ (APAC 2021 Top 50 CMO) and his philosophy of “Hiểu để yêu thương”: understand in order to love. Marketing isn’t a battle for attention. It’s a battle of moving people from mind to heart.
That means starting every project not with “how do we get people to buy?” but with “what struggle do they wake up worrying about?”
The campaigns I’m most proud of aren’t the ones that hit the biggest numbers. They’re the ones where someone reached out saying “I felt seen.”

reflections.title: Living Multiple Lives
paragraphs: You can’t write for people you’ve never talked to. So I try to live messily: eating at the phở stall next to the xe ôm drivers at 6am, then presenting to executives in an air-conditioned boardroom by noon.

The best insights don’t come from data dashboards. They come from overhearing a mom complain about her kid’s eating habits, or watching how a group of Gen Z friends roast each other’s fashion choices. Real life is the research.

reflections.title: Slowing Down to See Clearly
paragraphs: This industry moves fast and rewards people who move faster. I used to think speed was the game. Now I think clarity is.

The times I’ve messed up were times I was so busy proving I could deliver quickly that I didn’t stop to ask: “Is this actually what they need?” Humility isn’t weakness. It’s the only way to stay curious enough to keep learning.

philosophy.quote: “The best marketing doesn’t feel like marketing. It feels like someone finally understood what you were going through.”
philosophy.mission: I’m not chasing awards or viral moments. I’m trying to create work that makes people feel less alone. Work that gives them belonging, confidence, or just a moment of real connection.

afterwork.title: League of Legends
afterwork.subtitle: 10 years · Support main · Platinum
afterwork.description: The best players make everyone else look good. That’s support in a nutshell. Also: staying calm when the team is flaming at 11pm? That skill transfers directly to client crises.

afterwork.title: Guitar
afterwork.description: Self-taught. Still can’t nail barre chords. But choosing to be a beginner keeps you humble.
afterwork.takeaway: The discipline of being bad at something

afterwork.title: Cà phê sữa đá
afterwork.description: Morning. Afternoon. Evening. Sitting at vỉa hè stalls, watching Hanoi go by.

afterwork.title: Living with a cat
afterwork.subtitle: She runs the house, not me
afterwork.description: She ignores me when I want attention. Demands pets at the worst times. Zero respect for my deadlines. Perfect training for understanding that the world doesn’t revolve around your brand.
afterwork.takeaway: Your customer has a whole life going on

aboutCta.headline: Got a project? A question?
aboutCta.body: I’m always down for a good conversation. Especially if it involves marketing strategy, creative campaigns, or just a good casual story that you just remembered.
